heading: Obsolete material and articles of historical interest: loan or gift
label: 8765 Obsolete material and articles of historical interest: loan or gift
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7545.34 U.S.C. 546g, ; , .Aug. 7, 1946, ch. 804, § 260 Stat. 897Oct. 31, 1951, ch. 654, § 2(23)65 Stat. 707(last sentence as applicable to ).34 U.S.C. 546h34 U.S.C. 546g(last sentence as applicable to § 2), .Aug. 7, 1946, ch. 804, § 360 Stat. 897(1st sentence as applicable to , and 3d sentence).34 U.S.C. 546k34 U.S.C. 546g(1st sentence as applicable to § 2, and 3d sentence), .Aug. 7, 1946, ch. 804, § 660 Stat. 898In subsection (a) the words, “ordnance material” are substituted for the words “ordnance, guns, projectiles”. Posts of the Grand Army of the Republic are omitted from the list of authorized donees because there are no surviving members of that organization. The word “Commonwealth” is inserted to reflect the present status of Puerto Rico. Specific reference to the Canal Zone is omitted as unnecessary, since the Zone is a “possession of the United States” as defined in and is therefore covered by clause (1).section 101 of this titleSubsection (d) is added to note the existence of a later act, codified in , which provides similar disposal authority, and to give effect to , , which is not now contained in the U.S. Code, and which saves this section despite the apparent implied repeal.§ 2572 of this title§ 2 of the Act of February 27, 1948, ch. 7662 Stat. 37

(c). , as amended by , substituted “” for “section 205 of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 ()”.Pub. L. 107–217Pub. L. 107–314section 121 of title 4040 U.S.C. 4862001—Subsec. (a). , inserted heading and substituted introductory provisions for provisions which read as follows: “Subject to regulations under section 205 of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 (), the Secretary of the Navy, under regulations prescribed by him, may lend or give, without expense to the United States, captured, condemned, or obsolete ordnance material, books, manuscripts, works of art, drawings, plans, and models, other condemned or obsolete material, trophies, and flags, and other material of historic interest not needed by the Department of the Navy, to—”.Pub. L. 107–107, § 1043(a)(1)40 U.S.C. 486Subsec. (a)(1) to (12). , capitalized the first letter after the paragraph designation in each of pars. (1) to (12), substituted a period for a semicolon at end of pars. (1) to (10) and a period for “; or” at end of par. (11), substituted “a foreign war” for “World War I or World War II” in par. (5) and “servicemen’s monument” for “soldiers’ monument” in par. (6), and inserted “or memorial” after “museum” in par. (8).Pub. L. 107–107, § 1043(a)(2)Subsecs. (b), (c). , added subsecs. (b) and (c). Former subsecs. (b) and (c) redesignated (d) and (e), respectively.Pub. L. 107–107, § 1043(b)(2)Subsec. (d). , (c)(1), redesignated subsec. (b) as (d) and inserted heading.Pub. L. 107–107, § 1043(b)(1)Subsec. (e). , (c)(2), redesignated subsec. (c) as (e) and inserted heading.Pub. L. 107–107, § 1043(b)(1)Subsec. (f). , added subsec. (f).Pub. L. 107–107, § 1043(b)(3)1996—Subsecs. (c), (d). redesignated subsec. (d) as (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which read as follows: “No loan or gift under this section may be made unless—Pub. L. 104–106“(1) notice of the proposal to make the loan or gift is sent to Congress;“(2) 30 calendar days of continuous session of Congress have expired after the notice was sent to Congress; and“(3) during that 30-day period Congress does not pass a concurrent resolution stating in substance that it does not favor the proposed loan or gift.”1980—Subsec. (a). , substituted “section 205 of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 (),” for “”.Pub. L. 96–513, § 513(39)40 U.S.C. 486section 486 of title 40Subsec. (b). , substituted “section 3301” for “section 366”.Pub. L. 96–513, § 513(40)

children.heading: .—Authority To Make Loans and Gifts
children.label: a .—Authority To Make Loans and Gifts

children.text: A State, Commonwealth, or possession of the United States, or political subdivision or municipal corporation thereof.
An educational institution whose graduates or students fought in a foreign war.
A museum or memorial operated and maintained for educational purposes only, whose charter denies it the right to operate for profit.
A post of the Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States.
A post of the American Legion.
Any other recognized war veterans’ association.
A post of the Sons of Veterans Reserve.
Captured, condemned, or obsolete ordnance material.
Captured, condemned, or obsolete combat or shipboard material.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: c .—Regulations
children.text: A loan or gift made under this section shall be subject to regulations prescribed by the Secretary and to regulations under .section 121 of title 40

children.heading: .—Maintenance of the Records of the Government
children.label: d .—Maintenance of the Records of the Government
children.text: Records of the Government as defined in may not be disposed of under this section.section 3301 of title 44

children.heading: .—Alternative Authorities To Make Gifts or Loans
children.label: e .—Alternative Authorities To Make Gifts or Loans
children.text: If any disposition is authorized by this section and , the Secretary may make the gift or loan under either section.section 2572 of this title

children.heading: .—Authority To Transfer a Portion of a Vessel
children.label: f .—Authority To Transfer a Portion of a Vessel
children.text: The Secretary may lend, give, or otherwise transfer any portion of the hull or superstructure of a vessel stricken from the Naval Vessel Register and designated for scrapping to a qualified organization specified in subsection (a). The terms and conditions of an agreement for the transfer of a portion of a vessel under this section shall include a requirement that the transferee will maintain the material conveyed in a condition that will not diminish the historical value of the material or bring discredit upon the Navy.
